subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Future of Cosmology
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Future of Cosmology usable.

hook.text: Cosmology has answered huge questions in just a century. The next decades promise even bigger breakthroughs.

buildup.text: JWST is peering deeper than ever. The Vera Rubin Observatory will survey billions of galaxies starting in 2025.

discovery.text: Gravitational wave detectors may soon hear echoes from the Big Bang itself—primordial ripples in spacetime.

twist.text: We still don't know what dark matter or dark energy is. Solving either would be the discovery of the century.

climax.text: Cosmology's biggest questions remain open: What triggered the Big Bang? Is the universe infinite? Are we alone?

punchline.text: A century of answers. An eternity of questions still ahead.

quiz.question: What major unsolved problem could transform cosmology if answered?
options: Identifying what dark matter and dark energy actually are
Determining the exact age of the Earth
Mapping every star in the Milky Way
